reading.title: Local Communities & Artisans
reading.content:
Vong, a small village in Ha Noi, is famous for a speciality food: com (young sticky rice flakes). To make com, artisans follow a series of steps. They preserve the techniques by passing them down to their sons. In the past, people made com by hand, but now they use machines for some steps to shorten the process. They wrap the final product in two layers of leaves to preserve its fragrance before selling it to consumers. Com Lang Vong is well known in Viet Nam as a speciality of Ha Noi autumn.

Denby is a village in the county of Derbyshire, England. It is the home of the famous Denby Pottery, which is made from the finest local clay. Going around the village, we can see artisans hand-craft pottery collections. They still use some of the original techniques passed down through generations. In Denby products, we can see their beauty and function which make them world-famous. Today Denby is also a tourist attraction. Visitors can make pottery in workshops or find out about the history of pottery in the museum.

vocabulary.example: They preserve the techniques by passing them down to their sons.
They use machines for some steps to shorten the process.
They wrap the com in leaves to preserve its fragrance.
They still use some of the original techniques passed down through generations.
In Denby products, we can see their beauty and function.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: Factories use machines to ________ the production process.
We need to ________ our traditional crafts and culture.
The rose gave off a lovely and sweet ________.
What is the primary ________ of this new electronic device?

multipleChoice.question: What does the word 'original' mean in the passage?
How do artisans in Vong village keep their com making techniques alive?
options: By passing them down to their sons

multipleChoice.question: Which word means 'a pleasant sweet smell'?
Complete the sentence: 'This tool has many useful ________.'

reading.title: The Teenovator Competition
reading.content:
Teenovator is an annual competition by the City Teen Council. We ask teens to find problems of the city and suggest solutions. Here are this year's winners.

First place: The waste-free city by Central School. They see that the more developed a city is, the more food people throw away. They suggest carrying out a project which turns food waste into energy. Students would sort their leftovers at school canteens. A nearby farm would come to take the food waste and process it into biogas.

Second place: The teen-friendly city by Bookworm Team. This team feels that many public amenities, particularly the city library, are not teen-friendly. They suggest a list of changes so that the library can attract more teens. Some of them include designing lively learning spaces for teens and having teen's favourite desserts in the cafeteria.

Third place: The street-safe city by Helena Wilson. Helena realises that there are too many vehicles around school gates. This is unsafe for children who walk to school. She suggests that the city authorities not allow cars at school gates at drop-off and pick-up times. In addition, they should not let motorbikes park on the pavement near school gates.

vocabulary.example: Teenovator is an annual competition by the City Teen Council.
Students would sort their leftovers at school canteens.

vocabulary.meaning: Tiện ích công cộng, tiện nghi
vocabulary.example: This team feels that many public amenities are not teen-friendly.

vocabulary.meaning: Các nhà chức trách, chính quyền
vocabulary.example: She suggests that the city authorities do not allow cars at school gates.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: The local ________ decided to build a new playground.
Don't throw away your food; we can eat the ________ tomorrow.
She won first prize in the national writing ________.
The park has many good ________ like benches and toilets.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'Teenovator'?
Central School's project turns what into energy?
Which team suggested changes for the city library?
What does Helena Wilson suggest regarding school gates?

units.title: Unit 3: Healthy Living For Teens
units.topic: Lối sống lành mạnh cho thiếu niên

reading.title: Having a Well-Balanced Life
reading.content:
When I was at school, I had to learn how to have a well-balanced life in order to reduce stress and anxiety. Below are some of the typical things I did.

Firstly, I managed my time properly. I started to plan my schedule, made a weekly work list and gave priority to some of my work. This helped me concentrate my efforts on my most important tasks.

In addition, I communicated with my family, friends, and teachers about my busy schedule and problems, so they would offer me additional support.

I also took breaks appropriately because they helped me keep away from stress and anxiety, gave my brain a rest, and improved my mood.

Finally, I looked after my physical health. I got at least eight hours of sleep a day. I played football with my classmates twice a week. Besides, I also tried to avoid fattening foods like chips, cookies, and pizza.

vocabulary.meaning: Mối lo âu, sự lo lắng
vocabulary.example: I had to learn how to have a well-balanced life in order to reduce stress and anxiety.
This helped me concentrate my efforts on my most important tasks.
They offered me additional support when I was busy.

vocabulary.meaning: Một cách thích hợp, phù hợp
vocabulary.example: I took breaks appropriately because they helped me relax.

vocabulary.meaning: Dễ làm béo, chứa nhiều chất béo
vocabulary.example: I avoided fattening foods like chips, cookies, and pizza.

pairs.meaning: Mối lo âu, sự lo lắng

fillInTheBlank.sentence: Noise makes it difficult for me to ________ on my homework.

options: How to have a well-balanced life

multipleChoice.question: Which of the following helped the author keep away from stress?

reading.title: English Traditions & Cuisine
reading.content:
England's traditions have been around for hundreds, even thousands of years. English cuisine is among the deep-rooted traditions that English people are proud to keep alive.

Typical English cuisine has developed over many centuries, and people say that fish and chips is the most English dish of all. It is believed that fish and chips appeared in England in the 19th century. The earliest fish and chip shop opened in London during the 1860s. Since then people have considered fish and chips to be England's national dish, and it is now a common takeaway in the United Kingdom.

The basic ingredients of the dish are fried fish served with chips. People in different places may add peas, vinegar, lemon, or ketchup. Fish and chips is served hot as the main dish in England. Preserving and promoting fish and chips is the way English people keep themselves associated with the past.

vocabulary.meaning: Lâu đời, ăn sâu bén rễ
vocabulary.example: English cuisine is among the deep-rooted traditions of England.
It is believed that fish and chips appeared in England in the 19th century.

vocabulary.meaning: Món ăn mang đi, mua mang về
vocabulary.example: Fish and chips is now a common takeaway in the UK.
The basic ingredients of the dish are fried fish and chips.
Preserving this dish is a way to keep themselves associated with the past.

pairs.meaning: Lâu đời, ăn sâu bén rễ

fillInTheBlank.sentence: The sun ________ from behind the clouds.

multipleChoice.question: When did fish and chips first appear in England?

options: To keep themselves associated with the past
To make it healthier than other dishes

units.title: Unit 5: Our Experiences
units.topic: Những trải nghiệm của chúng ta

reading.title: Memorable Summer Courses
reading.content:
Duong: I had a hard 10-day course in an army camp in Son Tay last summer. Everything was different from my life at home. We had to wake up at 5 a.m. and attended classes which were like training courses for soldiers. In the evening, we read books or worked in teams. The team leaders walked us through many exciting activities. We also joined a performance that had the environment protection theme. We could only call our parents once a day. I have never attended such a strict but exciting course like this.

Akiko: I have been on an unforgettable summer course in America. I stayed in Thornwood campus on the outskirts of New York City for three weeks. We had an enjoyable campus tour, attended English classes, and joined team activities. The most special experience was my visit to the top of Rockefeller Centre. From there, I could view the whole city below. That was the first time I travelled without my parents, so I felt like I grew up a lot after the trip.

vocabulary.example: We joined a performance that had the environment protection theme.
I have never attended such a strict but exciting course like this.
I stayed in Thornwood campus on the outskirts of New York City.
The most special experience was my visit to the top of Rockefeller Centre.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: The university ________ has many old trees and modern buildings.

Our school has very ________ rules about wearing uniforms.
The ________ of this year's science exhibition is space exploration.

multipleChoice.question: Where did Duong spend his 10-day course last summer?
options: In an army camp in Son Tay

multipleChoice.question: How long did Akiko stay in America?
What does Duong say about his course rules?
options: It allowed them to call parents anytime

multipleChoice.question: Who did Akiko travel with during her trip to America?

units.title: Unit 6: Vietnamese Lifestyle: Then and Now
units.topic: Lối sống của người Việt: xưa và nay

reading.title: Learning Styles: Grandfather vs Granddaughter
reading.content:
Mr Nam, grandfather, a 70-year-old farmer: I was one of the few children in my village who was still at school at the age of 15. Every day, I got up early and walked to school. Learning then depended mostly on our teachers and textbooks. We had no library or lab. I learned simply by taking notes during class, memorising them, and doing the homework. We rarely travelled outside our village, so we didn't know much about the world around us.

Mai, granddaughter, a 15-year-old student: Learning today is very different from my grandfather's time. It is easier and more convenient. Besides learning from teachers, we use the Internet. It provides us with various online sources. Google helps us find the answers to almost any questions we have. The Internet also allows us to pursue our own interests. Learning has become more independent. We have TVs at home, and a library and computers at school.

vocabulary.example: I learned simply by taking notes and memorising them.
Learning today is easier and more convenient.
The Internet provides us with various online sources.
The Internet allows us to pursue our own interests.
Learning has become more independent today.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: Students need to become more ________ and study on their own.

Is it ________ for you to meet me at 5 p.m. today?
He wants to ________ a career in software engineering.

multipleChoice.question: How did Mr Nam learn when he was 15?
options: By studying online with a computer
By taking notes, memorising, and doing homework
By reading in the school lab

multipleChoice.question: According to Mai, what helps modern students find answers to almost any question?
What does the word 'independent' describe in Mai's passage?
Choose the word that fits: 'There are _____ ways to solve this math problem.'

units.title: Unit 7: Natural Wonders of the World
units.topic: Kỳ quan thiên nhiên thế giới

reading.title: The Dolomites - Paradise at Your Feet
reading.content:
The Dolomites are a mountain range in Italy. It was recognised as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2009. The Dolomites are part of the Alps, stretching from the Adige River to the Piave River valley. It covers an area of about 141,900 hectares. It has 18 peaks which rise above 3,000 metres. The mountain range is famous for its natural beauty, with steep rocky cliffs, sharp peaks, and narrow and deep valleys.

The area is a popular destination for tourists throughout the year. Visitors can enjoy winter sports such as skiing and mountain climbing, hiking, and cycling. There is also an annual bicycle race in the first week of July which covers seven mountain passes. This race occurs every year, attracting cyclists from all over the world to admire this majestic landscape.

vocabulary.example: The Dolomites are part of the Alps, stretching from the Adige to the Piave.
The mountain range has 18 peaks which rise above 3,000 metres.
The area is a popular destination for tourists throughout the year.
An annual bicycle race occurs in the first week of July.
Cyclists come to admire this majestic landscape.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: Vung Tau is a popular weekend ________ for people from Ho Chi Minh City.

multipleChoice.question: When were the Dolomites recognised as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?

Which word means 'happens'?

reading.title: Package Tours vs Self-guided Tours
reading.content:
Travelling has become a major part of modern life, with millions of travellers visiting new destinations every year. There are two primary ways people plan their journeys: package holidays and self-guided tours.

A package holiday is a pre-arranged trip where a travel agency organizes the itinerary, transport, and accommodation. This option is popular because it is convenient and reduces the stress of planning. The agency handles any problems that occur during the trip, which is helpful for older travellers or those who are not tech-savvy.

On the other hand, self-guided tours require travellers to work out everything on their own. They must search for a destination, estimate the cost, and buy their own tickets. For accommodation, they often hunt for cheap homestays. Although it requires more time and effort, this type of holiday is cheaper and gives travellers the freedom to change their schedule whenever they want.

vocabulary.meaning: Du khách, người đi du lịch
vocabulary.example: Millions of travellers visit new places every year.
A package holiday is a pre-arranged trip organized by an agency.
The travel agency organizes the itinerary and accommodation.
Self-guided tours require travellers to plan everything on their own.

vocabulary.meaning: Ước lượng, tính toán sơ bộ
vocabulary.example: They must search for a destination and estimate the cost.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: We should ________ the total cost before booking the flights.

Some ________ prefer staying in local homestays to learn about the culture.
I prefer a ________ tour because I love planning my own timetable.

multipleChoice.question: Who is a package holiday especially helpful for?
options: Older travellers or those not good with technology

options: The agency does all the work
They give travellers flexibility to change their schedule

multipleChoice.question: Which word means 'a planned route or journey plan'?

reading.title: The Three Circles of World Englishes
reading.content:
English has become the global language of communication, spoken by billions of people worldwide. To explain the different uses of English, linguist Braj Kachru proposed a model of three concentric circles in 1985.

The Inner Circle contains countries where English is spoken as a native or first language, such as the UK, the USA, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. In this circle, English is the primary language used in government, education, and daily life, and it provides the standards for other speakers.

The Outer Circle includes countries where English is not the native language but has official status as a second language, such as India, Singapore, and Nigeria. In these countries, English is used alongside local languages.

The Expanding Circle consists of countries where English is learned as a foreign language, such as Viet Nam, Japan, and Brazil. In this circle, English is used mainly for international communication, and speakers follow the standards of the Inner Circle. Because of the spread of English, there are now many varieties of English spoken worldwide.

vocabulary.example: Braj Kachru proposed a model of three concentric circles.
The Inner Circle contains countries where English is the native language.
English has official status as a second language in India.
In the Expanding Circle, English is learned as a foreign language.

vocabulary.meaning: Các biến thể, sự đa dạng
vocabulary.example: There are now many varieties of English spoken worldwide.

pairs.meaning: Các biến thể, sự đa dạng

It is not used at all

multipleChoice.question: What does the word 'varieties' mean in this text?

reading.title: Flora, Fauna & Ecological Balance
reading.content:
Earth is home to a rich variety of flora and fauna, which work together to maintain the ecological balance. Plants, or flora, absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen through photosynthesis, which is essential for human and animal survival. Animals, or fauna, consume plants and other animals, and their waste acts as fertilizer to enrich the soil.

However, human activities like deforestation, pollution, and climate change are threatening this ecological balance. Many species of plants and animals are now endangered.

Fortunately, there are several conservation success stories around the world. In the USA, the bald eagle was saved from extinction by banning harmful chemicals and protecting its habitat. In Rwanda, mountain gorilla populations have recovered due to local community efforts. In India, wild tiger numbers have increased through strict anti-poaching laws. These stories prove that we can protect our planet if we take action to preserve our flora and fauna.

vocabulary.example: Flora and fauna work together to maintain the ecological balance.

vocabulary.meaning: Hệ thực vật (tất cả các loài thực vật)
vocabulary.example: We must study the local flora of this mountain forest.

vocabulary.meaning: Hệ động vật (tất cả các loài động vật)
vocabulary.example: The island is home to a rich and unique desert fauna.
Fortunately, there are several conservation success stories around the world.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: The Amazon rainforest is famous for its diverse ________ and fauna.

multipleChoice.question: What is the primary role of flora (plants) in the atmosphere?
options: They absorb carbon dioxide and release oxygen

multipleChoice.question: Which chemical ban helped save the bald eagle in the USA?

options: By using machines to shorten processes

multipleChoice.question: What is 'fauna'?
options: All the plants of an area
All the animals of an area

reading.title: Technology in Modern Classrooms
reading.content:
In modern classrooms, electronic devices have changed the way students learn and interact. Leaflets from schools recommend several tools to make studying more effective.

An e-reader is a portable electronic device designed specifically for reading digital books. It is lightweight, can store thousands of books, and has a screen that is easy on the eyes. Students no longer have to carry heavy textbooks to school.

A smart projector is another useful device. It allows teachers to display high-quality images, interactive presentations, and videos directly from their laptops. This makes lessons more visual and interesting.

Finally, many schools use 3D printers in science and design classes. A 3D printer allows students to create physical objects from digital models, helping them understand complex engineering concepts. While these devices are expensive, they offer great benefits for education.

vocabulary.example: An e-reader is designed specifically for reading digital books.

vocabulary.meaning: Dễ dàng mang theo, di động
vocabulary.example: The e-reader is a portable device that fits in a small bag.
A smart projector displays slides directly from the laptop.

fillInTheBlank.sentence: I bought an ________ because I love reading but hate carrying heavy books.
A laptop is ________, so you can easily work in a cafe or on a train.
The teacher turned on the ________ to show a video of a volcanic eruption.
My new ________ can store over five thousand novels.

multipleChoice.question: Why is an e-reader good for students' eyes?
options: It has a screen designed specifically for comfortable reading

multipleChoice.question: What does a 3D printer allow students to do?
options: Create physical objects from digital models

multipleChoice.question: Which word means 'able to be easily carried or moved'?
How does a smart projector connect to show slides?

reading.title: Changes in the World of Work
reading.content:
The world of work is changing rapidly due to technological developments, creating new challenges and career choices for the future. Many repetitive jobs that involve simple, routine tasks are being replaced by smart machines and software.

For example, some receptionist and cashier jobs are now automated, as customers can use self-service screens. However, this shift does not mean there will be no jobs. Instead, technology is creating new career opportunities.

There is a high demand for programmers, data analysts, and digital marketers who can build and manage these new systems. Additionally, personal service jobs that require human empathy, like nail artists, hairdressers, and therapists, are growing. When choosing a career, young people should focus on developing skills that machines cannot copy, such as critical thinking, creativity, and communication.

vocabulary.example: Modern technology creates new challenges and career choices for the future.

vocabulary.meaning: Lặp đi lặp lại (gây nhàm chán)
vocabulary.example: Many repetitive jobs that involve routine tasks are being replaced by machines.
Some receptionist and cashier jobs are now automated.
There is a high demand for programmers who can build these new systems.

multipleChoice.question: Why are some cashier and receptionist jobs being replaced?
options: Because they can be automated by smart machines

multipleChoice.question: Which of the following fields has a high demand for jobs in the future?

multipleChoice.question: Which word means 'doing the same thing over and over again'?
